signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + NFP Shock
signals.text: US June Nonfarm Payrolls print at +57k vs +110k expected, triggering a broad USD sell-off and driving the G10 leader-to-laggard spread to a critical 1.345% gap. GBP leads G10 performance (+0.677% 1W average across 4 pairs) while USD lags (-0.668% 1W average across 7 pairs). The macroeconomic shock of the jobs miss eases Fed rate hike fears, overriding the neutral OIS carry differential (SOFR 3.68% vs SONIA 3.73% — -5bp favoring GBP). GBP/USD trades at 1.3343, supported by a positive GBP economic surprise index (CESI net +24 beats) vs a stalling USD data regime. The options market remains cautious, with 25d risk reversals at -0.80 showing a moderate put skew, indicating hedging against a rapid GBP pullback despite the spot breakout. Watch for a sustained break above 1.3400 to confirm structural USD weakness, or next week's US CPI release to invalidate the soft-landing narrative.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Technical + Options Skew at Key Level
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 161.023, holding just above the critical 160.00 threshold that historically triggers Bank of Japan verbal and physical intervention. Despite a massive +270bp OIS carry advantage favoring the USD (SOFR 3.68% vs TONA 0.98%), the pair fell -0.92% intraday following the weak US jobs report. JPY speculative positioning remains heavily short with COT LF net short at -115,033 (28.4% long), presenting severe short-squeeze risk if US yields continue to decline. The options market is aggressively pricing downside protection, with the USD/JPY 25d risk reversal at -1.09 (moderate put skew) and an elevated Vol Risk Premium of +3.3% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.9%). Watch for a daily close below 160.00 to signal a structural trend reversal and trigger a rapid unwind of crowded carry positions.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4179, showing a stark divergence between institutional and retail positioning. CFTC COT data shows leveraged funds are heavily net short CAD at -87,096 (20.6% long), which translates to structural USD/CAD long exposure. Conversely, retail sentiment is at an extreme 92% short on USD/CAD, actively fading the institutional trend. This extreme retail short positioning creates a contrarian risk of a further squeeze higher if USD/CAD breaks key resistance. However, the US jobs miss acts as a near-term headwind, and the USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken significantly to -0.871 vs its 252-day norm of -0.033 (z = -1.94σ), meaning CAD is failing to benefit from oil price dynamics. Watch for retail capitulation or a break of 1.4250 to confirm the next leg higher.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Risk Premium Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.6921, up +0.34% intraday as the weak US jobs report softens the USD. The options market is pricing an extreme Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of +17.2%, with implied volatility at 23.9% vs a realized 30-day volatility of just 6.7%. This massive premium indicates intense hedging demand and fear of downside risk, which is further reflected in the 25d risk reversal of -0.89 (moderate put skew). This options caution contrasts with bullish speculative positioning, where COT LF net is long +29,035 (64.9% long). The RBA holds a nominal rate of 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%, providing a -67bp OIS carry advantage to the AUD (SOFR 3.68% vs AONIA 4.35%). Watch for a break above 0.7000 to shift the bearish options bias, or the upcoming RBA meeting on 11 Aug to redefine the carry narrative.
